items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama knows how to fix the NBA's 65-game rule
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama knows how to fix the NBA's 65-game rule — Wembanyama shared his honest thoughts on the 65-game rule, whether it works and if it should be changed or removed.

items.headline: Nuggets To Sit Most Of Rotation Sunday; Nikola Jokic Questionable With Award Eligibility At Stake
items.blurb: Nuggets To Sit Most Of Rotation Sunday; Nikola Jokic Questionable With Award Eligibility At Stake — The Denver Nuggets again ruled out most of their regular rotation Sunday against the San Antonio Spurs, but three-time MVP Nikola Jokic remains questionable. Jokic needs at least 15 minutes to secure eligibility for postseason awards. Denver enters Sunday as the No. 3 seed in the Western Conference

items.headline: Keaton Wagler Declares For 2026 NBA Draft
items.blurb: Keaton Wagler Declares For 2026 NBA Draft — Illinois guard Keaton Wagler has declared as an Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. Wagler is currently ranked fifth on ESPN's Top 100 prospects for this year's draft. Wagler played in 37 games in his lone season for the Fighting Illini. The 6-foot-6 guard averaged 17.9 points, 5.1 rebounds and 4.2 assists as a freshman. Wagler also put up 44.5

items.headline: Raptors Convert AJ Lawson To Standard Contract
items.blurb: Raptors Convert AJ Lawson To Standard Contract — The Toronto Raptors are converting wing A.J. Lawson from his two-way contract to a standard contract for the remainder of the season. This will make Lawson eligible for the postseason for Toronto. Lawson has appeared in 23 games for Toronto this season. The 6-foot-6 wing has averaged 4.0 points in 9.4 minutes per game for the Raptors. Lawson has sho

items.headline: Dusty May Agrees To New Deal With Michigan
items.blurb: Dusty May Agrees To New Deal With Michigan — Michigan coach Dusty May has agreed on a deal that will have him continuing to lead the Wolverines "for many years to come," athletic director Warde Manuel said Saturday. Manuel made the comment during a ceremony celebrating Michigan's NCAA tournament championship. "Dusty and I have already reached an agreement," Manuel said in a remark that resulted in

items.headline: Stephen Curry Tweaks Ankle In Loss To Kings, Expects To Play Sunday
items.blurb: Stephen Curry Tweaks Ankle In Loss To Kings, Expects To Play Sunday — Stephen Curry suffered an ankle scare Friday during the Golden State Warriors' 124-118 loss to the Sacramento Kings, but expects to be healthy for Wednesday's 9/10 Play-In game, which will likely be against the Los Angeles Clippers. The 38-year-old guard slipped on a wet spot on the floor with just over nine minutes remaining in

items.headline: Satou Sabally, Jonquel Jones Sign Multiyear Deals With Liberty
items.blurb: Satou Sabally, Jonquel Jones Sign Multiyear Deals With Liberty — Satou Sabally has agreed to a multiyear deal with the New York Liberty, her agent Zack Miller of WME Basketball confirmed to ESPN. Center Jonquel Jones also agreed to return to New York on a multiyear contract, a separate source confirmed. Sabally's decision was driven by New York's championship culture and her belief the Liberty rem

items.headline: Road to the Playoffs: Raptors chasing finale win to secure playoff berth
items.blurb: Road to the Playoffs: Raptors chasing finale win to secure playoff berth — The final nine playoff spots will be determined Sunday when all 30 teams are in action. That includes the Raptors, who need a win in the finale to avoid the Play-In, writes Zulfi Sheikh.

items.headline: Basketball Intelligence For 4/11/26
items.blurb: Basketball Intelligence For 4/11/26 — Today's Best NBA Reporting And Analysis

items.headline: Dalano Banton, Celtics Agree To Two-Year Contract
items.blurb: Dalano Banton, Celtics Agree To Two-Year Contract — The Boston Celtics are signing guard Dalano Banton to a two-year contract. Banton will fill Boston's 15th standard roster spot going into the playoffs. The Celtics timed things down to the day to fill out their roster, while also finishing the season under the luxury tax. Boston will avoid the tax by about $11 thousand dollars. That, combined wit

items.headline: Hayden Gray, Jazz Signing Two-Year Contract
items.blurb: Hayden Gray, Jazz Signing Two-Year Contract — The Utah Jazz are signing guard Hayden Gray to a two-year contract. Gray spent this season playing for the Maine Celtics of the G League. Gray appeared in 48 games for Maine with 23 starts. The 6-foot-4 guard averaged 9.9 points, 2.8 rebounds, 5.8 assists and 2.1 steals in 27.9 minutes per game. This signing will bring the Jazz back to the maximum of 1

items.headline: Nuggets extend winning streak despite resting starters vs. Thunder
items.blurb: Nuggets extend winning streak despite resting starters vs. Thunder — The Denver Nuggets didn't care enough about playoff seeding to play any of their starters Friday night. After the Oklahoma City Thunder sat nine rotation players themselves, they came away with their 11th straight game and clinched home-court advantage in the first round.

items.headline: A winning season doesn't mean job security for Trail Blazers leadership
items.blurb: A winning season doesn't mean job security for Trail Blazers leadership — The Portland Trail Blazers are bound for the play-in tournament this season, their first postseason appearance in five years. That success might not translate to job security for the people in charge.

items.headline: 'Two cerebral basketball players': Lakers' LeBron James addresses connection with Luke Kennard
items.blurb: 'Two cerebral basketball players': Lakers' LeBron James addresses connection with Luke Kennard — The real story centered on the connection between James and Kennard, a pairing that continues to develop as the Lakers navigate through roster limitations.

items.headline: OG Anunoby Exits Knicks Win Early Due To Left Ankle Injury
items.blurb: OG Anunoby Exits Knicks Win Early Due To Left Ankle Injury — The New York Knicks wrapped up the #3 seed in the Eastern Conference Playoffs with a win over the Toronto Raptors on Friday. However, that might have been a costly victory for New York, as OG Anunoby suffered a left ankle injury. Anunoby tripped over Scottie Barnes while contesting a shot early in the second quarter. The Knicks forward a

items.headline: Swarm Win G League Finals; Tosan Evbuomwan Named MVP
items.blurb: Swarm Win G League Finals; Tosan Evbuomwan Named MVP — The Greensboro Swarm won the G League title for the first time in franchise history. The Swarm defeated the Stockton Kings 2-0 in the best-of-three G League Finals. Forward Tosan Evbuomwan was named MVP after scoring 22 points in the clinching game. Evbuomwan is on a two-way contract with the Swam and the Charlotte Hornets.

items.headline: Amid late-season chaos, Raptors’ Temple finding peace of mind on greens
items.blurb: Amid late-season chaos, Raptors’ Temple finding peace of mind on greens — During the tumult that comes with the final days of the NBA season, there is always one reliable oasis of calm for Garrett Temple: A Saturday off to watch The Masters. Michael Grange tees it up with the vet.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama Scores 40 Points In Game He Only Played To Meet 65-Game Award Rule
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama Scores 40 Points In Game He Only Played To Meet 65-Game Award Rule — Victor Wembanyama played in Friday's game against the Dallas Mavericks after suffering an injury earlier in the week for the primary purpose of reaching the 65-game threshold required for regular season award eligibility. He accomplished that, came out of the game but then returned, finishing with 40 points and

items.headline: Cedric Coward: Won't play Sunday
items.blurb: Cedric Coward: Won't play Sunday — Coward (back) has been ruled out for Sunday's game against Houston. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Mark Williams: Questionable for Sunday
items.blurb: Mark Williams: Questionable for Sunday — Williams (foot) is listed as questionable for Sunday's game against the Thunder. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Collin Gillespie: Iffy to face OKC
items.blurb: Collin Gillespie: Iffy to face OKC — Gillespie is listed as questionable for Sunday's game against the Thunder due to a right shoulder contusion. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jalen Green: Iffy for Sunday
items.blurb: Jalen Green: Iffy for Sunday — Green is listed as questionable for Sunday's game against the Thunder due to right knee soreness. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards: Sitting out Sunday's game
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards: Sitting out Sunday's game — Edwards won't play Sunday against the Pelicans due to right knee injury maintenance. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Joel Embiid Out Of Hospital, No Timetable For Return
items.blurb: Joel Embiid Out Of Hospital, No Timetable For Return — Joel Embiid was discharged from a Houston-area hospital on Friday after undergoing an appendectomy on Thursday. There is no timetable for Embiid's return. "It is a tough blow," coach Nick Nurse said. "I think that the biggest thing right now is that Joel comes out of the surgery as best as possible and all that goes well. That's where most of

items.headline: LeBron James reaches 12,000 assists as Lakers trounce Suns
items.blurb: LeBron James reaches 12,000 assists as Lakers trounce Suns — LeBron James had 28 points, 12 assists and six rebounds, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers clinched home-court advantage in the first round of the playoffs with their 101-73 victory over the Phoenix Suns on Friday night.

items.headline: Heat Convert Jahmir Young To Standard NBA Contract
items.blurb: Heat Convert Jahmir Young To Standard NBA Contract — The Miami Heat are converting Jahmir Young's two-way contract to a two-year, standard NBA deal, sources told Shams Charania of ESPN. The Heat waived Terry Rozier earlier on Friday, creating the roster spot for Young. Young's salary for next season will be non-guaranteed. Young will now be eligible for the postseason.

items.headline: Brittney Griner Set To Join Sun On Seven-Figure Deal
items.blurb: Brittney Griner Set To Join Sun On Seven-Figure Deal — Brittney Griner is finalizing a seven-figure deal with the Connecticut Sun, sources told Alexa Philippou of ESPN. Griner, who had spent her entire career with the Mercury, averaged 9.8 points and 5.2 rebounds for the Dream last season. The 35-year-old is a six-time WNBA All-Star. The team is set to move to Houston for the 2027 season. Griner i

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Lakers 101, Suns 73
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Lakers 101, Suns 73 — LeBron James led the way with a 28-point, 12-assist double-double and Luke Kennard had 19 points as the Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Phoenix Suns 101-73.

items.headline: Why Kendrick Perkins is still not buying the Knicks after win over Celtics
items.blurb: Why Kendrick Perkins is still not buying the Knicks after win over Celtics — Former NBA champion with the Celtics and current ESPN analyst Kendrick Perkins was on Friday's edition of "First Take" and made it clear he is still not a believer in the Knicks overtaking the Celtics in the Eastern Conference.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns help Knicks clinch No. 3 seed
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns help Knicks clinch No. 3 seed — As the NBA playoff field takes shape in the final days of the regular season, the New York Knicks now officially know their place.

items.headline: Celtics tie their own NBA record vs. Pelicans
items.blurb: Celtics tie their own NBA record vs. Pelicans — Two teams in NBA history had ever made 29 three-pointers in a game before this week. Friday night, the Boston Celtics became the second team in five nights to tie the single-game record for threes.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Kings 124, Warriors 118
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Kings 124, Warriors 118 — Devin Carter knocked down six three’s and finished with 29 points, while Maxime Raynaud added 23 points and nine rebounds as the Sacramento Kings edged the Golden State Warriors 124-118. Brandin Podziemski poured in a game-high 30 points in the loss.

items.headline: Warriors facing a dilemma heading into NBA postseason
items.blurb: Warriors facing a dilemma heading into NBA postseason — The Golden State Warriors face a unique dilemma of their own heading into the play-in tournament.

items.headline: Tiered lottery proposal could solve NBA's tanking problem
items.blurb: Tiered lottery proposal could solve NBA's tanking problem — NBA commissioner Adam Silver has proposed changes to the league's draft lottery to discourage teams from losing on purpose. Now there's a new wrinkle that could dramatically change the incentives for losing.

items.headline: 'Where's Woj?': Doc Rivers calls out Shams Charania over Bucks reporting
items.blurb: 'Where's Woj?': Doc Rivers calls out Shams Charania over Bucks reporting — Rivers didn't appreciate a recent report from Charania.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 116, Clippers 97
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 116, Clippers 97 — Deni Avdija dropped a game-high 35 points and Donovan Clingan added an 18-point, 13-rebound double-double to lead the Portland Trail Blazers past the Los Angeles Clippers 116-97.

items.headline: Notable update emerges on Victor Wembanyama's injury
items.blurb: Notable update emerges on Victor Wembanyama's injury — The 22-year-old Wembanyama went down during Monday’s win over the Philadelphia 76ers.

items.headline: NASCAR icon Dale Earnhardt Jr. makes his pick for NBA Rookie of the Year: 'He deserves it'
items.blurb: NASCAR icon Dale Earnhardt Jr. makes his pick for NBA Rookie of the Year: 'He deserves it' — NASCAR Hall of Famer and North Carolina native Dale Earnhardt Jr. is paying attention to the NBA Rookie of the Year race.

items.headline: Luka Doncic makes unexpected move following unfortunate Lakers injury news
items.blurb: Luka Doncic makes unexpected move following unfortunate Lakers injury news — The Los Angeles Lakers are dealing with a ticking clock when it comes to Luka Doncic and his injured hamstring.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Timberwolves 136, Rockets 132
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Timberwolves 136, Rockets 132 — Anthony Edwards dropped 22 points and Terrance Shannon added 23 points to help the Minnesota Timberwolves beat the Houston Rockets 136-132.

items.headline: NBA Roundup: Pistons surge past Hornets, locking Charlotte into play-in
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: Pistons surge past Hornets, locking Charlotte into play-in

items.headline: LeBron James makes NBA history with son Bronny in Lakers' win
items.blurb: LeBron James makes NBA history with son Bronny in Lakers' win — Of his many big moments and accolades, LeBron James will probably remember one play from Thursday night's game as much as any when he's long retired.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Jazz 147, Grizzlies 101
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Jazz 147, Grizzlies 101 — Seven players scored in double figures, led by Ben Hinson’s 30 points, while Bez Mbeng and John Konchar each recorded triple-doubles off the bench as the Utah Jazz trounced the Memphis Grizzlies 147–101.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 127, Thunder 107
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 127, Thunder 107 — Jonas Valanciunas led the way with a 23-point, 17-rebound double-double and David Robby chipped in 21 points off the bench to help the Denver Nuggets beat the Oklahoma City Thunder 127-107.

items.headline: Raptors Takeaways: Larger goal still within reach despite loss to Knicks
items.blurb: Raptors Takeaways: Larger goal still within reach despite loss to Knicks — Friday’s outcome was predictable for the Raptors, who were missing several key contributors and have now lost to the Knicks in 13 straight meetings.

items.headline: Wembanyama has 40 points, 13 rebounds in 65th game to lead Spurs past Mavericks
items.blurb: Wembanyama has 40 points, 13 rebounds in 65th game to lead Spurs past Mavericks — Victor Wembanyama had 40 points and 13 rebounds in his 65th game to become eligible for NBA awards, and the San Antonio Spurs beat the Dallas Mavericks 139-120 on Friday night.

items.headline: Nneka Ogwumike Chooses Sparks Over Lynx In Free Agency
items.blurb: Nneka Ogwumike Chooses Sparks Over Lynx In Free Agency — Nneka Ogwumike has agreed to rejoin the Los Angeles Sparks after two seasons with the Seattle Storm. The news was first reported by her sister, Chiney Ogwumike, an ESPN analyst. Ogwumike also visited the Minnesota Lynx before settling on a return to Los Angeles as she enters her 15th professional season. Ogwumike is a 10-time All-Star. The v

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Magic 127, Bulls 103
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Magic 127, Bulls 103 — Franz Wagner dropped a game-high 25 points and Jamal Cain chipped in 20 points off the bench to help the Orlando Magic beat the Chicago Bulls 127-103.
